crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Detects imphash often found in malware samples (Maximum 0,25% hits with search for 'imphash:x p:0' on Virustotal) = 99,75% hits
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: Arnim Rupp (https://github.com/ruppde)

attributes.magic: PE32 executable (GUI) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The sample is a malicious loader or crypter designed to unpack and execute a hidden payload. Key indicators include multiple RWX (Read-Write-Execute) segments, non-standard section names (.hello, .world, .pack), and a startup routine that manually resolves the IAT (Import Address Table) using LoadLibraryA and GetProcAddress. The binary contains a cryptographic implementation (sub_6719155a and sub_671916ae) that resembles the AES block cipher, used for decrypting the payload in memory. After decryption and relocation (sub_671914c7), the program performs a 'tail jump' to the Original Entry Point (OEP), a classic technique to bypass static analysis.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects the presence of a number of Windows API functionality often seen within embedded executables. When this signature alerts on an executable, it is not an indication of malicious behavior. However, if seen firing in other file types, deeper investigation may be warranted.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs
